name: Adams, Arthur J.
motto: Jack was always a glutton for punishment.
description: Chemical engineering has as yet failed to discourage him, and he has added that gentle art of warfare to his activities. Besides juggling test tubes in the laboratory, he may be seen on the baseball diamond tossing around the "pill," in which sport he won his numerals as a freshman.

Chemical engineering has as yet failed to discourage him, and he has added that gentle art of warfare to his activities. Besides juggling test tubes in the laboratory, he may be seen on the baseball diamond tossing around the "pill," in which sport he won his numerals as a freshman.

name: Adams, Robert Cuthbert
motto: Bob should be elected the best looking R. O. T. C. officer in the Junior class.
description: He certainly looks well in that uniform. "Bob," sometimes called "Cut-throat," has attractions in a nearby Massachusetts city which cause him to spend many hours acting like "Joe Gondon." That's all right, "Bob," now that your favorite Boston "dive" is closed it won't be so bad.

name: Agrafiotis, Peter J.
motto: As individuals we often wonder whether Peter is a real Greek name and the reason for our wonderment - well- there was a certain old Hebrew who accompanied Christ when he climbed the mountains, and boy, let us remind you, Peter's chief failing is the mountains and nothing else but-.
description: Why Peter, - I can't pronounce his last name, but get the main idea - "Pete" is a darn good man in whatever he undertakes, in spite of his general descension to the proverbial dogs. And don't forget... Peter put in a lot of hard work on the GRANITE and The New Hampshire.

name: Aldrich, Edward
motto: Neil, one of our enterprising Chemical Engineers, is one of the boys who can always be heard around the Chem. Lab. from 8:00 A.M. to midnight.
description: Cheerful or serious, he is always ready to help out when the freshmen get stuck. Before long, we expect to see his name on the list of the best of chemists. And say, can't he swallow Climax!

name: Allsworth, Clayton R.
description: From the moment "Clayt" struck our campus he became an acknowledged "jack-of-all-trades." Such versatility has gained for him abundant fame among his classmates. In fact, so widely known has he become to certain elements on the campus that they chose him Vice-President of the Student Council last spring, so that he might air his liberal opinions to the student body. However, his activities do not interfere with his non-stop flights to Branford.

name: Ames, F. Clement
description: When one thinks of "Clem," one thinks of engineers and mains. In spite of the fact that "Clem" tops off an average around 90 now and then, he finds plenty of time to enjoy the other side of college life. "Clem" even found time last fall to investigate the mysteries of that glorious game called "Michigan." "Clem" is hardly a "Bookworm," he is just a fellow who wants to enjoy college, and get something out of it.

name: Annett, Dorothy A.
description: In diminutive "Dot" is concentrated enough energy and enthusiasm to make - or break - the University, but we have to share her bounding spirits with South Berwick and all points between there and Durham. Dancing and debating are her diversions, or vocations - it's hard to say which. Anyway, she's a great success in both.

name: Atwood, George L.
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Secretary (2), Treasurer (3)
description: "Joe" has transformed co-education from a minor subject to one of the fine arts. As champion bush-jumper and rug-hopper, G. Loyd has left a trail of broken hearts from Berlin to New Rochelle, including such way stations as Smith Hall and Claremont. Cribbage in "Joe's" eyes is one of the greatest of the minor sports, and for a quiet evening's entertainment "Joe" excels in this thrilling game. It is hard to find out why, because the only thing he'll say is "You Peg here, and you Peg there, you know." It must be great "Peggin'" around.

name: Avery, Harold W.
motto: Hal has never been heard to say an unnecessary word, but still waters run deep.
description: As he talks but little, he gives but an equal amount of knowledge about himself. At Manchester High, he was chosen as the quietest fellow in his class. He certainly deserves the same title here, but "Hal" believes in storing up his energy for the future. "Then watch my smoke," says he.

name: Ayer, Theodore
description: We are wondering if "Ted" will ever spend a week-end on the campus. It must be that he has interests elsewhere. "Ted" has a habit of avoiding eight o'clocks. We wonder why? As an all-round sport and a true friend "Ted" commands a high mark.

name: Babbitt, Harold Marshall
description: Babbitt and Noyes are seen together as much as that famous pair, "Ham and Eggs." One is lost without the other. "Bab" is one of "Ma" Leighton's hash slingers, and the freshmen say that if he likes you, you may get an extra dessert. "Bab" likes college very much; he's having a great time and figures that when his four years are up, he'll be rested enough to go to work.

name: Bakeman, Madelene Edson
motto: The cellos, setting forth apart, Grumbled and sang.
description: "Scrado" is an important member of the Ballard Hall Orchestra. Though usually very quiet, she has been known to shock her friends by her vivid, if not very wicked language. A good friend, a pleasant companion, that's "Scrado!"

name: Baker, Margaret
description: Fortunate indeed is the person who can be called a blossom in Peg's heart. She is hard to get acquainted with—even those who know her best don't always understand her—but once you do know her, she's the best sport ever. She is an all-round girl as shown by her activities and by her ability to shark certain courses.

name: Barker, Mary Haywood
description: College life is such a thrill that Mary has had to develop a poker face to keep the sparks from bursting forth. And now even her English profs think that she is bored! Dancing, novels, poetry, and munching Sundaes take up most of her time, although Mary dreamed of being an equestrienne until a fracas with an apple tree frustrated her hopes.

name: Batchelder, Charles E.
description: "Batch" comes from New Hampshire's seaport city, but his interests do not run along naval lines. Ask any of the boys at the house what "Batch's" chief dissipation is, and you will discover that he is a well known detective. On account of his quiet, unassuming attitude, his great achievements are unknown to the common populace.

name: Batchelder, Leon Wallace
description: "Len," unlike most of us, has had very good reasons for leading the straight and narrow path while in school. The reason? Well, isn't Durham his home town? Reason enough, then, we'd say. But he manages to leave the ol' fireside once in a while, and then—oh, boy!

name: Arnold, Bauby
description: A good sport and a good pal, ready for anything, anywhere, anytime. We know "Bob" will be successful as a lawyer, for he sure does love to argue and win "cases." May the best of luck be yours in whatever you do, "Bob."

name: Elizabeth, Blum Bauer
description: "Betty" is one of those girls who can do everything well - drag 88 averages, play, sing, dance, and act. What more can one expect from such a tiny person? Her brown eyes, her cheery smile, and her bewitching dimple make friends for her rapidly. Everyone loves "Betty" for her cute ways, her magnetic personality, and her strong character.

name: Maurice, Elmer Bickford
motto: "No, ma'am, that isn't an advertisement for Arrow Collars - that's 'Stub.'"
description: "Stub" believes that "a letter a day keeps the doctor away," and that this world revolves around Ithaca, N. Y. To prove that "Stub's" personality has nation-wide prominence, one should read the offers that he has received from such towns as Chicago and Ithaca, N. Y., providing he will settle in their community after graduation from college.

name: Paul, Henry Blaisdell
description: "One for cribbage!" "Let's play Remo!" "Yes, I told you to call me at six o'clock but I'm going to sleep now for six minutes." (Snores.) "Soap" does get up for an eight o'clock once in a while. He also finds time between naps to see that the Glee Club gets along all right, that The New Hampshire receives hot write-ups, and that Mask and Dagger has an actor. Moulin Rouge, Mil. Art Hop., Certified checks??

name: Blake, Jane Elizabeth
description: Jane's life is a social whirl, and if you want to know anything about love affairs and their solutions come to Jane. But that isn't all - as Woman's Editor of The New Hampshire, GRANITE Historian, athlete, and scholar, she is equally proficient. Bridge is her specialty just now.

name: Block, Dorothy C.
motto: "Late to bed and early to rise; She's bashful, studious, humorous and wise."
description: With "Dot" it's a case of "The Daughter's Saturday Night." She studies unless there's a Durham letter to answer. She's athletic too, - or why the Manager's pin? "Dot's" favorite colors are orange and black - three guesses why!

name: Bodwell, Bradley
description: We gaze in the crystal and - Presto! Twenty years have passed. "Brad" is seated in a lavishly furnished brokerage office dressed, as usual, absolutely "comme il faut." He is dictating an article for Vanity Fair, to a very beautiful stenographer. Incidentally, the title of it is, "What the Well-Dressed Man Should Wear." Then, turning to his secretary, he orders her to write out a check for U. N. H. for having turned out such a great man.

name: Bourque, Joseph E.
description: The gang always wondered where Elinor Glyn got her inspiration for "IT" until "Burky" oozed onto the campus three short years ago. Though the answer to a maiden's prayer, he rarely ever smiles at the co-eds for we all know that he's in Liberal Arts for Art's sake. A corking scout - Stay with 'em "Joe!" Your intentions are good.

name: Bowden, George James
motto: Good things come in small packages, and if 'Skinney' has not always been good, he has been awful, awful careful.
description: As a chemist he knows how to smash up apparatus as well as the next man and with a pipe he is very strong. You might expect to find "Skinney" at the bottom of the heap in a scrimmage, but instead he is always the reciprocal thereof. He may not be chairman of the tubbing committee, but he has helped a few to find their way to the shower room. Here's to the best of luck with the test tubes, "Skinney."

name: Breck, Mary Elizabeth
motto: She's pretty to walk with, Witty to talk with, And pleasant to think on.
description: Mary's winning smile will get her into heaven - if nothing else will. When driving, her constant question is "Do I hear a speed cop behind me?" Although she has not joined the ranks of the bobbed-haired bandits, she certainly can do almost everything else - and do it well.

name: Britton, Marjorie L.
motto: "Marge" is eternally loyal to egg sandwiches, dancing, and weekend parties.
description: And what a drag she has with the Profs! She can make two nineties stand where one zero stood before. We also recommend her for high honors in the Campustry Department - it seems that her favorite song has changed from "Lullaby from Joslin" to "Big, Bad Bill."

name: Brown, Charles
motto: I'll fix it up for you and she is a Wow.
description: "Rod" came down with a great "rep" as a sheik back home. His only problem is which suit to wear. Between the Delta Pi House and the Chi Omega Home there is a well worn path - ask "Rod," he knows. He is just a case of another local boy making good.

name: Brown, Elizabeth Frances
motto: Don't mind me — I work here
description: "That's 'Betty.' Always busy at something. She's a 'Reg'-ular fan when it comes to football. Can she curl hair? Ask the girls! The F. L. T. on that ring makes us wish you the greatest of success in your 'home-ec' profession, 'Betty'."

name: Brown, Florence M.
motto: A charming stranger came among us.
description: "Flos" transferred from Texas, but one doesn't know what that means until they meet the quiet little girl who has joined us this year. She showed her interest and ability in dramatics in "Dear Brutus" last term. Her voice is a welcome addition to our glee club and she insists upon spending some time on athletics for athletic's sake.

name: Brown, Fred H.
description: Fred is the "Man of Mystery" — he disappears every Friday and turns up Monday morning, usually half an hour late for his eight o'clock. No one knows where he goes or why. Rumor has it that he used to go to Portland every week-end, but of course that is nothing but "idle rumor" and Fred is far from idle wherever he is. However, "Brownie" is very modest in all his doings, and he manages to pull down a good average every term.

name: Brown, Ralph A.
description: "Buckin Jim," alias "Strategy," alias "Jumper"; Brown, is "Tick Tack Too" champion of Murkland Hall and cribbage shark extraordinary. "Brownie" will debate for hours at a time on the relative athletic prowess of Vermont and New Hampshire prep. schools. We are told that "Brownie" lapses into unconsciousness for short periods, but when he is his normal self, you can find under his external coat of "Bull throwing" a good all around fellow.

name: Bruce, Robert E.
description: Robert Edmund, product of the north country, ambled down from Ashland along with the inseparable "Don." Failing to be impressed by the co-eds, they disappeared without ceremony, and were at last discovered in Pennsylvania, headed for parts unknown. Upon the persuasion of fond parents, "Bob" returned to play varsity basketball, and put the shot for the varsity track team. He has since become famous as a "roads scholar," bummer, and student.

name: Buffum, Edward H.
motto: "Straightforward, fearless, frank and unpretentious, his was a true friendship, unalloyed by ulterior motives."
description: The above would have gained "Eddie" a lasting place in our affections, but when supplemented by a nature as generous as it was frank, his character commanded approbation. Already well versed in many of the requisites of a physician through long and persistent pharmaceutical studies, we are certain that when "Ed" has completed his medical course he will be a boon to his profession as well as a godsend to humanity.

name: Bujnievicz, Charles Anthony
description: "Chuck," the fair, from the windy city of Laconia, found his way to learning in the great class of '29. Being all ambition, he cast his lot with the boy chemists and has crashed his glassware with the rest. Taking extreme delight in the gentle art of debating, he has acted as manager for those who like to argue. "Beney" also knows the good and bad of pictures and has acted as student photographer during his stay here.

name: Burnham, Edward P.
description: "Ed" better known as "E. P." drove into Durham in a "Packard" in '25 and next year will go out in a Ford. As a Chemist he is not; as an Educator he is not; but as an Engineer - "Ed" sure knows his stuff. Nashua! - Yes, he receives mail from his home town every day - she is nice!

name: Cahalan, Earl Francis
description: Some day when you find "Cal" in good humor, ask him his full name. Believe it or not, they say it is Earl F. D. J. W. Q. Cahalan. Rather a long handle for such a small fellow. "Cal" is one of New Hampshire's versatile cross country and track men, and everybody will agree that he has certainly filled in a creditable way the shoes of the renowned "Duke" Peaslee. A freshman aspirant for Cross Country honors once yelled at him, "One side, Goggles," and the most that he has seen of "Cal" since then has been his heels.

name: Caie, Thomas Arthur
description: "Art" blew in from Berlin with his snowshoes on his back, expecting to find the southern section of the state, notably Durham, covered with snow. He hasn't been able yet to figure out why it doesn't snow here in September as it does in the great open spaces, where men are men and women manage to keep warm. He changed his course last year to I. E. "Art" says this was for the good of the course, but from reports emanating from Concord, where he was stationed, we believe there's one "femme" in the case.

name: Campbell, Celia Storrs
description: Celia, with that deep voice, so soft and low, is the girl with the irresistible giggle, the best pal ever, a whiz at bridge, and how she loves to dance! Once the opera claimed her, but she decides now that the world would rather have a feminine athlete - the type of girl who doesn't mind "Jack-ing" up a Ford.

name: Campbell, David Robert
description: Dave has not been with us long. He transferred from Northeastern University, and we all feel that he is quite an asset to the class of '29. Dave has a serious outlook on life and works hard as is shown by his record here in school. His hobby is athletics, and much of his time is spent either on the football field or in the gym watching the N. H. teams practice. Only on rare occasions is he caught smiling. Come on, "Bob," stretch that face into a smile.

name: Chadwick, William L.
description: He's a bit dark to look at, but he shines in Accounting. Between his work at the Commons, Cross Country, and studying he does not have time for much trapping.

name: Churchill, Randolph Emerson
description: A big car, pretty girl, bright lights and a big city — "Ran's" idea of Paradise. "Ran" is the social lion of the campus; his headquarters at present are at the Copley-Plaza, and trips to Boston week-ends are in order. "The Dover Flash," as he is known in athletic circles, talked his way into the University — and how! His silver-tongued orations rival Daniel Webster's, although we think that his success is due more to his plentitude of "IT."

name: Cheney, Marian Louise
description: A true blue friend, a pal worth having — one who cheers you when you're blue and is always ready to lend you a helping hand — that is Marian. Although she is a Latin major, and an excellent student, she seems to get some fun out of college. She is always smiling no matter what the weather, and always has a kind word for the discouraged.

name: Child, Elizabeth L.
motto: To be or not to be: that is the question:
description: This from our Beth who invariably isn't in to telephone calls. She is always ready for a good time as well as the more serious phases of college life. "Beth" is one of our hardest workers. She is a loyal supporter of The New Hampshire and Granite Staffs. Her talents are not all literary, however, as shown by her ability on the athletic field.

name: Clark, Justin M.
description: Not so long ago "Jud" complained of having too much spare time on his hands. Inasmuch as he is taking a chemical engineering course the matter is very serious and should be looked into. On coming to college, "Jud" dropped athletic activities and gave his attention to class presidency and the Student Council. If he succeeds in getting through college without pulverizing himself by attempting to divide atoms, we look forward to hearing things from him in later life.

name: Clement, William
description: "No, Genevieve, that is not a cowboy coming down the street, that is 'Foolish Felix!'" How did he get warped so? Why, when he was a little boy he used to stand before a music stand practising on his fiddle and the strain was too great. Three years ago, "Felix" hitched up the dog team and left his native haunts for the milder climate of Durham, where he has become something of an athlete. He says that the moustache is not an attempt to disguise himself, he is just covering up.

name: Cleveland, Alton A.
description: Days may come and days may go, but the friendships of "Buddy's" will go on for ever. Never without a smile and a cheery word of greeting, always willing to help when help is needed, Alton has made a host of friends on every section of the campus. He is not divided in his choices either. The opposite sex play a very important part in his actions, and why shouldn't they? "Buddy" has a stride that will carry him far in life's hard race.

name: Cleveland, Esther M.
motto: In ourselves our fortune lies, Life is what we make it.
description: Esther comes from Plymouth Normal and although she is a newcomer, we are sure that only time is needed to make her one of us. Already the flash of her blond hair as she hurries from Madbury Road to campus is a familiar sight.

name: Hurley, E. Cloutman
description: After winning all non-stop dance contests in Conway, in quest of new and larger fields, came to New Hampshire where he has not stopped dancing as yet. His lasting smile, his curly hair, and his grace on the dance floor can't help but voice the question in every co-ed's mind, but as yet they are none the wiser.

name: Thomas, William Colby
description: Bill fell out in Durham from a Haverhill hayrack one day, determined to succeed or die in the attempt. He signed up for the Technology course mistaking it for agricultural. Although he talks like a farmer, he certainly has a technical mind. Finding that the engineering course did not give him enough to do, he decided to take up a few outside activities. Just look them over!

name: Margaret, M. Connor
motto: Good things come in small packages
description: It takes to prove it. Of course she's a good student, but what we appreciate most is her good disposition and natural wit. And musical - her fingers are as nimble as her tongue. She is the orchestra at the Congreve Hall Dinner Dances. We never see her going out with any of the Durham men but what about the driver of the little green car that comes down occasionally?

name: Walter, W. Cook
description: Cookie has not been with us long. Last year Dad or some other kind-hearted person gave him a room in Dover, a Ford to commute with, and an insurance agency to play with. He dropped the insurance game and his rough rides from Durham to Dover, settling down to study. Thus a quiet, studious, innocent-appearing youth we have among us.

name: Mildred, V. Corey
motto: Hockey, tennis, basketball, Swimming, and clogging too — The arts in which our 'Mil' excels Are not so very few. She kicks, she runs, she even yells (Her lungs are good as new!) But when it comes right to the point Our 'Mil' is just true-blue.

name: Margaret, L. Cournoyer
motto: To know her is to love her.
description: "Peg" comes from East Jaffrey. We never heard of the place but we do know "Peg," who never misses a dance or a high average. Aside from her social activities, "Peg" can "parlez," sing, and "bring home the bacon" in advertising.

name: Edward, W. Crawford
description: This is "IT" personified! "IT" may seem quiet, but — you don't room with him! His week-ends are far from weak in the Port City — and Revere isn't so far away! "Ed" has a "fine" time driving — all the speed cops know him. And speaking of motoring, it's a lie about his roller skating home from that Nashua ride. Marks come easy to "Ed"; four of his five courses are in music. Between "times" he leads the orchestra, murders harmony with the rest of the Glee Club, plays the organ, and lives up to his reputation of being a prince.

name: Bruce, Cromwell
description: "Chesty" hails from the wilds of Woodsville. We don't know whether he developed that gait tramping through the woods up there or not. He is a wow on the soccer field and if you don't believe it, ask anyone who has played against him. Some day he will be a great forester if he keeps on the way he is working now. They say that there is some attraction down at Jackson. That probably accounts for his not giving the co-eds any time. Good luck, "Chesty," and look out for forest fires.

name: Cummings, Dane P.
motto: All hail! the flaxen-haired son of Peterborough is among us. Attention men! Here is your opportunity to get a line on the coming styles without buying a copy of Vanity Fair. 'Jake' is a good scout and a hard worker even though he has a passion for good clothes. 'Jake's' many love affairs have been featured in the country's leading popular literature. The leading epigram tacked after his name is: 'Stalwart Jake - the Answer to a Maiden's Prayer.'

name: Cummings, Melbourne Wesley
motto: If you can keep your head, when all about you Are losing theirs and blaming it on you.
description: That's "Mel." When a fellow, and a busy Tech Student at that, takes over a job half done and does it - when he also takes care of the advertising for the "New Hampshire" and manages a freshman sport - I guess we'll agree with Kipling. "Mel" broke his pipe last term and we all missed it a lot but he probably wouldn't have had much time to smoke it anyway.

name: Dahlberg, Marjorie
motto: A jaunty step, a cheery smile, a merry twinkling eye.
description: Never so busy when you're blue but that she tries to comfort you. Always ready for some fun, yet she does her share when work is to be done. Her strong personality, her ideals, her many talents, make her outstanding; a girl everyone admires, a true blue friend and an all around good pal - that is our "Marge."

name: Davis, Burnham Burrowes
motto: Whoopee! The boy from the great, wild north. One can easily visualize him hurtling from precipice to precipice in a little tin Lizzie. 'Burnie' says that the only way a man can keep warm in his native haunts is to go farther north. 'Burnie's' ambition is to become a lawyer, and we say that if his legal tactics match his voice, he will be a 'wow.' A great big heart with a personality to match it - that's 'Burnie.'

name: Gordon, Decker
description: "Deck" is one of the boys who keep ol' Tom in the business. Although one of the Fairchild hall room boys, he spends all his spare time in the pool parlor. An unusually quiet lad, he prefers the "lone wolf" game. As is characteristic of all engineers, he burns the midnight oil expecting that some day he will be building bridges. Make sure they are not "bridges of sighs," Gordon lad.

name: John, Decourcy
description: A straight and stalwart youth is he who hails from the "Queen City" where love does not go a-wanting. "Ramrod's" good looks and manly appearance make him one of the most popular folks on the campus. In other words, "He's an Answer to a Maiden's Prayer". Too bad, though, girls - not a chance in the world - his attentions are bent toward other goals.

name: Real, Des Rochers
description: For three long years you have faithfully "differentiated," "integrated," and "analyzed" - and yet you have survived! Night escapades were very rarely outlined for you. A woman-hater, some say, but we know better, Real, and we, also, prefer brunettes. Keep up the fine record; we are confident you will break up the atoms and discover many new elements in years to come.

name: Margaret, Valentina Dicey
description: Does one inherit instincts for sociability from one's ancestors? We quote the case of the Spanish forebears who have left a name like Marguerita Valentina, and who have endowed "Peg" with the characteristics that have made her so famous on campus. Was it from these ancestors that came the ability to manage any kind of a party, dinner, rush, Irish, flowery, freshman, "box"?

name: Dillingham, Marjorie
motto: A true friend, like unto no other
description: "Marge" is so quiet and unassuming that we usually feel her presence rather than hear her, but then 'tis an accomplishment to be quiet and reserved. Her chief interest lies in the fancy skating of a certain pre-med. She has a friendliness, unselfish and sincere, thus life for her will keep its gladness ever growing and she will find this world worth while, we are sure.

name: Dimock, Winona
description: "Naidie" is partial to Scotch but that didn't hinder her from commuting last Spring - in fact it almost caused U. N. H. the loss of one of its fair co-eds. Gentlemen may prefer blonds but some girls prefer red, and as an after-thought, just who started this football - basketball fad anyway? Only those fully initiated to "I Patta" could understand or appreciate it.

name: Donnelly, John
description: Treading a wearisome trail for three years from Dover to Durham, "Johnnie" is still "trekking" along with us for that ol' pigskin. He is one of the "distinguished for service" in the Technical department. As a steady worker he has few equals, and boy, wait 'til he gets going!

name: Dow, Gordon Sumner
description: Washed up from North Hampton astride the back of a snail, "Sam" came to college to become a C. P. A. Alas! this has come to be only a side issue, for a large part of his time he spends managing the soccer team and studying the construction of Congreve Hall. In spite of these, "Sam" hits his courses hard and whatever his purpose may be we look for him to come out on top.

name: Dow, John Henry
description: We are all glad that U. N. H. won this budding genius away from Earl Leiderman's Correspondence Course, but even now "Johnny" is often seen admiring his hero of former days - the great Earl. John's popularity has been won by his two extremities - his voice and his feet. Anyone who has ever seen him do his trick dancing or heard his melodious voice can understand why this social butterfly is so much in demand.

name: Downing, Roger H.
description: Roger is another of those boys who started in well as an engineer, but decided that it was too hard work, and so changed to Liberal Arts. He hasn't quite decided whether he'll live in Durham next term, or commute from Newmarket. But all joking aside, "Rog," we wish you the best of luck now and forever, Amen.

name: Duncan, Merial Louise
description: Not Scotch, but economical. Not wound up, just a woman. Not fat, though always eating. Not a diamond, but we're sure there will be. This is "Mim," and all's said that needs be.

name: Dunlap, Lloyd W.
description: This "fair haired" classmate makes frequent week-end trips to Laconia; the explanation of these trips he has not yet disclosed. Let's hope it's nothing serious. "Dun" likes to sit on the steps of Ham's Grocery Store and take in the sun. Guess that's where he gets his sunny disposition.

name: Dustin, F. Carleton
description: A pair of skis and a field of snow, that's paradise for Frank. He came from Penacook to become an engineer and he may fool us and be one yet. In the winter his time is spent on skis, on which he has made himself well known. He is a quiet fellow and although he says the co-eds do not rate any time with him, yet we wonder why an engineer has to spend so much time at the library.

name: Eaton, Clyde S.
motto: "What's for Supper, Clyde?" "Hamburg steak, of course."
description: Clyde put the coffee pot on for two years at the poultry plant, and he's now keeping house in the "castle" of the plum orchard. He ought to make somebody a good husband. We know he is one of "Red" Richardson's best poultrymen, but why did Professor Fuller send him to Tennessee to judge dairy cattle?

name: Ekstrom, Lillian
motto: "Lil loves her swimming, A frolic in the snow, A tune to dance by, And a boat to row."
description: And can she sling? Yes! Hash or tennis racquets. We all know "Lil" for her musical giggle and cheery smile.

name: Eldridge, Gertrude A.
description: "Trudie" is an outstanding example of a good disposition and a healthy constitution. "Trudie's" good nature and helpfulness is appreciated by all who know her. Nothing much ever worries her, least of all such unnecessary things as classes, and her favorite expression in dismissing all such annoyances is: "Who cares about that?"

name: Smith Elwood, Guilford
description: "Guil" is one of the best fellows on the campus, even if he does come from Pinkerton. He never blows his own trumpet, but says it with a saxophone. Being a poultryman he is very much interested in chickens, especially one from Keene Normal School. Maybe that is the reason the co-eds have so little attraction for him. "Guil" made his choice early. "There's a reason," and we think it is not due to grape-nuts either.

name: Evans, John Beecher
description: Stone and Webster nearly went out of business when John decided that Forestry needed his services more than the engineering profession. No doubt his change of heart was due to the fact that his home town is situated up where the wildcats play tag on Main Street. He is so engrossed in his work that he finds it necessary to walk through the woods "alone" every afternoon.

name: Farrell, Lyle H.
description: "Lil" rooms with two very exceptional young men, but they just can't keep up with him. He has been active in football, boxing, baseball, dramatics, debating, and music and is now turning his attention to the only sport left — co-eds. He has started with the freshman class, but may give them all a turn later. Farrell is next year's football captain but in baseball he is usually out in left field.

name: Fay, Charles R.
description: One more Keene lad making good at New Hampshire. "Chick" is a quiet, good-natured boy and a willing worker. Though he spends most of his time in the engineering buildings and at his studies, his smiling face has gained him a reputation as an actor on the local stage. How such a good-looking boy can escape the co-eds has often puzzled us. We suspect the answer is in the girl he left behind him.
text: One more Keene lad making good at New Hampshire. "Chick" is a quiet, good-natured boy and a willing worker. Though he spends most of his time in the engineering buildings and at his studies, his smiling face has gained him a reputation as an actor on the local stage. How such a good-looking boy can escape the co-eds has often puzzled us. We suspect the answer is in the girl he left behind him.

name: Fenton, Paul J.
motto: I'm all right, but how did it happen?
description: Paul is working on the assumption that 4-H Clubs and Ayrshire cattle are the salvation of New Hampshire's agriculture; and no one has ever been able to change his mind. He claims his residence is in Andover, and says that both he and the folks vote there. Willing to help, always kind, liked by all, and with high ideals — that's Paul.

name: Fish, Charles Richard
clubs: First Sergeant, R. O. T. C.
description: Professor Perley's right hand man. For three years, "Dick" has beaten a straight and narrow path from Conant Hall to the Gym (via Congreve). As assistant manager of the Winter Sports team, he caddies for Coach Perley's extra ski poles. He is a favorite with his test tube associates, even though he makes a lot of breaks. We predict a brilliant career for "Dick."

name: Fish, Theodore
description: "Ted" puts studies first in his college life and is constantly muttering something about getting at those lines and graphs. He is probably better known as the "man behind the counter" at Jack Grant's. Although he is taking an engineering course at present, we wouldn't be surprised to see him break into the lunch room game one of these days.

name: Fitzgerald, Leo
description: Athletics at Dover High have looked up since the school was fortunate enough to secure the services of Coach Fitzgerald. The football team won a few games formerly — now it doesn't win at all! "Fitzie" is a product of the Rockne System, having followed the horses for a long time in his youth. It is rumored that "Fitzie" will be the next head coach of Woodman Institute of Taxidermy, but he refuses to comment on the proposed position.

name: Fleming, John Daly
clubs: R. O. T. C., Battalion Sergeant-Major (2)
description: "Jack" is, beyond all doubt, one of the biggest men on the campus. He is big in every way. He can make the biggest story from the smallest framework of anyone in school. Aside from being a story teller, "Jack" is editor of the school paper and also furnishes the audiences at Mask and Dagger plays with many a laugh. "Jack" knows most of the girls on the campus but he generally spends most of his time away from Durham at the BOWERY.

name: Flynn, Richard
description: "Dick" hails from Dover. He's hot, but he doesn't smoke. You see, since he has been under the tutelage of "Butcher" Lynch he's just had to keep rigid training. Just let him tell about those fights in the shade of the old apple tree. More like wrestling matches if you should ask the on-lookers - that's just his style of fighting. But what is the use of talking - you know "Diamond Dick," the "Man in the Moon."

name: Fosburg, David L.
description: "Dave" is a great "Molly" coddler. Since making his home in Durham he has worn a beaten path toward Dover and while he is on that glorious trail he goes under the name of "Bright Eyes." Soccer and Winter Sports found him a staunch man - good work "Dave." As a systematizer he has no equal. "Dave" is taking a five-year course, signing up for thirty-eight units a term.

name: Fowler, Clarence A.
description: Clarence is another hard-working engineer, but for all that, he manages to 'hit' Art Stewart's Movie course for a nearly perfect average. "Fowl" is a darned nice chap, and although he does chauffeur for H. P. Hood & Co. in the summer, we think he'll make a fine engineer. He doesn't say much about girls, but he hears from one in New York with pleasing regularity.

name: Fowler, Ralph L.
description: Late in the fall of 1925 there burst in upon this serene college, fresh from conquests of the north, (Holderness!) a small demure lad known as Tony. After three years, what have we? A popular boy, who, though perhaps not known to all the girls, is known and liked by all the fellows. He came here to study and learn, he tells us, and so he has, for he has taken all the courses available and is still willing to take more. When not puffing on some old cigar stub, he distinguishes himself as a basketball player and a dashing halfback for his fraternity.

name: Francis, Horace Brown
description: Hobie is one of the men about campus who is never without a girl. We'd like to know just how he does it. Hobie runs the picture-framing business about town and does various other things so that it is possible for him to go out with a girl and still make both ends meet. Good work, Hobie, keep it up.

name: Frye, Gilman V.
description: Gil comes from the city of Franklin and he wants everyone to know it. He started college with aspirations of becoming an engineer, but he has decided that Accounting and Economics have more in store for him than Mathematics and Physics. Cribbage is one of his favorite pastimes. We wonder if being unlucky at cards means anything. Gil is possessed of a perfect disposition, and all who know him cannot help but like him.

name: Fuller, H. Ellsworth
description: Whoopee for Nashua! May all her loyal sons be as popular as Al. To have the 1927 Mil. Art Camp in Nashua proved to be just too bad for this bashful man. He had to come back to school for a rest. Al has a host of friends, and his wonderful personality is responsible, as many of the fair co-eds have found out.

name: Gadbois, Irene Louise
description: Curly hair, a flash of brown eyes, a low soft voice, and Irene, with a speed that left the peppiest of her athletic competitors far behind her, won the praise and envy of all the '29 girls. Perhaps because of her excessive athletic activity, or possibly because she "fell" while skating one night, she developed severe "heart trouble," and we see she is now wearing a little diamond-shaped fraternity pin.

name: Garland, James Hill
description: We understand from a very reliable source that "Jim" was rated as one of the prettiest and cutest children reared in the nearby town of Raymond. Another report has it that he was found in one of the caves on Pawtuckaway Mountain. Be that as it may, Jim has developed into one of the best "Blister Rust" men in New Hampshire if not in the East. If you don't believe it - any time you have a couple of hours to spare ask him about it. He is also the best carpenter that ever used a second-handed plane.

name: Garlock, Ralph M.
description: After visiting half the prep schools in New England, Ralph decided to grace our campus with his august presence. He is now official judge of the last word in women. He knows 'em - and how! When Ralph is not entertaining with his singing he is cutting a mean figure on the glacial court. His knees are not really out of joint, it is natural for him to walk that way.

name: Gee, Warren H.
description: We nominate for the Hall of Fame "Austin" Gee, for the book he is now preparing entitled "The Trials and Tribulations of an Engineer." All went well with "Austin" until this year when he found attractions in Dover. It is said that if "Austin" thought less about women and more about Physics he would have a 90 average. Don't misunderstand; "Austin" is just a good scout trying to get along.

name: Gienty, Edward K.
motto: A letter a day will keep other girls away,
description: So seem to be the thoughts of some fair, young school teacher, far away in the hills of South Danbury. "Ted's" good natured attitude makes him an ideal target for Dr. Bowler's jokes. "Ted" recently brought forth, to heavy sleepers, a theory stating that two alarm clocks will work much more satisfactorily than one. But only a pail of ice water will do the trick for "Teddie." Ask his room mate — he knows.

name: Gilbert, Arthur
description: "Gillie" is a Pre-Med student as we all know. We can always hear him humming that well-known song, called "Margie." We hope that he has learned it all by now. Say, if you want to see yourself impersonated, just go to him, he sure knows how to twist himself. We all envy his ability as a skater and as a twirler of the drum-sticks, and wish him success.

name: Gilchrist, Marshall W.
description: "Gil," the pleasant boy with the pleasant smile, has more jokes than a millionaire has five-cent pieces. If you want to hear the latest "Barber Shop Fable" call on "Gil." He knows them all and can tell them as no one else can. "Gil" will never hurt himself hurrying, but he manages to get there on time — occasionally.

name: Gillette, Charles W.
description: "Freckles," the boy with the way — and such a way, with certain of the so-called lady friends. Back in 1925 there came to Durham another wishing to go the way of all chemists. In the good old daze, when everything but silence reigned and studies never ruled, "Freckles" got into lots of trouble. He just wouldn't be quiet and let the boy barrackers study. At last, he nearly fell in love and so did not return to the books the following fall. Once more he has entered our midst and is working hard again with the other inmates in Conant Hall.

name: Gillingham, Elizabeth
description: We concede to Elizabeth a supreme quiet dignity. Yet it is such a friendly quality in her that we can find no limit to the classes of her friends. Her chief hobby is helping "Dad" straighten out all our snares at the registrar's office- a truly noble one. Although always busy, one wonders at the spare time she seems to have when help is needed. Yes, Elizabeth is "there," and we are more than glad that she is also "here."

name: Glidden, Kenneth E.
description: "Ken" makes us all envious when the marks come out. Any "Chem" student who gets an average of above 90% deserves plenty of credit. The funny part of it is, he doesn't seem to study much, it just comes to him. "Ken" is a fine fellow, and we all wish him the best of luck.

name: Goodhue, Carmel Irene
description: And here is Miss Hospitality herself. "Rene," we're mighty glad you transferred from Southern. May we always be around when you hand out your apples and toast! Although your constant "shushing" told us that the noisy freshies were a nuisance, your "whispering tenor" proves they were not too burdensome. So here's to you, "Rene," toast and apples, cheerful smile, and all!

name: Goodwin, Crystal Evelyn
description: "Buddie" commutes from the nearby city of Dover and is proud of it. We haven't become fully acquainted with "Buddie" because of her absence from school during our first two years, but we soon got to know her. Her ready smile and cheerful "Hello" make her a popular girl, and she's a good sport and a pal to all. Just ask her to do a favor and see if she refuses.

name: Googins, Robert Wendell
description: Googins — brown hair, parted in middle; ivory-rimmed glasses; far-seeing eyes; thick lips, Ethiopian; scrawny neck; neatly-pressed blue serge suit, immaculate; glistening shoes; dark repository of secrets — Tulkinghorn, Googins — history; theosophy; medicine, tinctura aconiti, mandragora; university catalogues, Wittenberg, Porto Rico; J. Fletcher; melancholia — Jaques. Googins — the unappreciated, ab-normis sapiens.

name: Gray, Beatrice A.
motto: How doth the busy little bee?
description: "Bee" is industry itself — studying, making the soccer team, or hemming someone's dress for the dance. She's the "friend in need" we've heard about — always ready to help. Her patience is endless. "A snooze a day" is Bee's motto, and it usually comes after supper. She's a quiet girl, but a good athlete and a bright scholar.

name: Gulumian, Hrant Garabed
description: Hail the "Prince of Morocco"! A "Prince" in every sense of the word is "Gloomy." What's his hobby? Well— we'll say he is a fine judge of women, and when it comes to dancing, — "the kid's there." What else need be said — why, nothing. Stick with us "Gloomy," boy.

name: Guptill, Alexander L.
motto: I don't want to grow up.
description: The "Peter Pan" of the Junior Class, personifying everlasting youth. "Gup" started with the class of 1928, but changed his mind. Finding life unbearable without the "one and only" he spent two dollars for a license, and at present he and his family reside on Madbury Road. The results of his domestic training were demonstrated in the hotel at Springfield where he insisted on making his bed every morning. His scholastic record has been variable but since his marriage he's been hitting 'em for a million.

name: Gustafson, Walter Ludwig
description: Sometime within a few years after Walter gets through college we expect to hear that the world is being burned up as a result of a book published on a hundred ways to make money. "Gus" surely has tried that many ways already and has been more successful than some people would think. If "Gus" is going to enlighten us that way, he might also tell us how he made that letter in varsity soccer.

name: Hall, Herbert
description: Now here is "Herbie!" "But where?" we ask. Under the buggy robe, or possibly he intends it to be a fur coat. Something to keep him warm - but he is hot as it is. Hot on a trail, which trail we cannot find. Cracking books is easy for "Herbie," but he rates a bit better on cracking jokes. A peach of a fellow and so easy to find. Just look for a bush of whiskers or a "Fascisti" shirt and "Herb" will be nigh. Girls, do not fail to get acquainted!

name: Handy, Iva Louise
description: Iva's sincerity and friendliness make her a wonderful pal. She is an all-round good sport. Some of her specialties are playing tennis, making her eight o'clocks on time, and helping to make the third floor of Congreve interesting. References - any of the third floor gang. And we mustn't forget her "Sax" which is one big asset(?).

name: Hare, Richard M.
description: We fooled you "Dick." "Dick" has been playing around the Aggie farm campus for quite a while, and he thought he could give us the "slip." A hard worker, a good marker, and a sure success. What is it to be, "Dick," dairy business? We wish you prosperity, but watch out for "them" cows.

name: Harriman, Donald R.
motto: Hi Bird!
description: Truer words than these have never been uttered. "Oh, I think Don Harriman is just wonderful!" Don is a school teacher by choice, athlete by ability, scholar by necessity, and social lion by love. "Donnie" throws several things well, one of them being the basketball, for he is one of the fastest varsity forwards. There are no dances held in Eliot, Farmington, or Gonic unless "Don" is notified. They just wouldn't be a success without him.

name: Harriot, Donald
clubs: Y. M. C. A. Freshman Cabinet (1)
description: "Don" has spent his time between Jack Grant's and Concord, and anyone who knows why, can see why, he does go to Concord. Lately "Don" changed bosses and now is under "Ma" Leighton's power, and, according to him, he likes it. He always did like the women anyway, whether it's "Ma" or "Sugar mamma." "Red Hot" Harriot, that's he. George Olsen could probably get some hot stuff for his music if Harriot should happen down that way sometime.

name: Harris, Eleanor Woodward
description: The words "dignity" and "Eleanor Harris" are synonymous. As much as she dislikes to be catalogued under such a term, we are sure she will always be associated with just that quality. Even her contagious laugh and the occasional twinkle in her eyes do not detract from her sedateness. And that is not all, for Eleanor plays hockey and acts equally well. A loyal, honest friend, unselfish and lovable, is Eleanor.

name: Harris, Kora
description: It is a known fact that potatoes are raised in Maine, but in Kora we have evidence that peaches, not potatoes, are the product of the Pine Tree State. In the past, Kora's interests in languages were centered on Latin, but lately they have changed to Italian, probably because of her constant association with "Wop."

name: Hartshorn, George Main
description: G. Main was one of the hardest men to see concerning this little sketch. "Why have a writeup?", said he. "My picture will tell volumes." Undoubtedly so, "Main," but you needn't be so bashful. The least we can say, old boy, is that we'll miss your pleasing smile when the time comes for us to part.

name: Hatch, John Knight
clubs: Color Sergeant, R. O. T. C.
description: "Jack" is another of our neighbor juniors, commuting daily from Dover, one of the suburbs of Durham. However, we see more of him lately since he has developed a liking for, — say, "Jack," didn't Hardy write a novel about "a pair of blue eyes?" Quite a little is said these days about the life of a commuter, but "Jack" manages to give some time to track in the spring.

name: Hayford, John E. Jr.
description: "Ahoy, there!" John Hayford, Jr., Economist, Accountant, and heartbreaker par excellence is speaking. And when he speaks he generally says something. One day back in the fall of 1925, John dropped off a train at the Durham station, squared off, and proceeded to knock his studies for the proverbial loop. For three years in succession he has taken Art Stewart's Movie Course, which he seldom "cuts."

name: Haynes, Virginia Porter
motto: Take some honey from a bee - mix it up with T.N.T. - and what have you got?
description: "Ginnie" is the personification, colorful and animated, of the "Keep Smiling" adage, apart from being an eternal advertisement for dental creams. A miss with a "soul," and a "Red," warm, vibrant one too! "Ginnie" is a blonde of the type not only preferred by gentlemen, but also by the rest of the world.

name: Hirsch, Charlotte
description: Versatile? I'll say! Scholarship, dramatics, sports, executive ability, dancing and social life. However, the world cannot dream of the weighty affairs discussed in Charlotte's private office at the drug store. Her democratic spirit has made her beloved and respected by a large circle of friends.

name: Hobbs, Paul W.
description: Paul Wesley, monarch of North Hampton and all points East arrived in the city of Durham one bright morning and filed his intentions of becoming a great "butter and egg man." Indications point toward his succeeding in this line, as we often hear from some distant point that he is collecting prizes for his work on judging teams representing the college.

name: Hoch, Linford F.
description: "Lin" was not with us for a long time. It must be that having been a traffic cop down in Delaware he had a certain amount of roving blood instilled in his veins. We will always remember him for his remarkably sunny disposition and his southern accent to which it was such a pleasure to listen.

name: Hodgdon, Melville
description: He's always worrying about passing, but he always gets there. He is quiet and unassuming, but let him get in a wood-lot, then he makes his trees grow straight and tall as all nice young trees should. "Mel" commutes from Dover, unfortunately, so we see little of him on the campus.

name: Houghton, Clarence Winthrop
description: The emblems on the shields of the "Knights of Old" have nothing on the emblem of our fair blond pre-med student. Many know of his pet jack "Nolie," with the anchor dangling from her neck. We wonder why "Wink" always picks out a small room-mate. It's all right as long as you don't overwork them, "Wink." By the way, "Wink's" room-mate follows him everywhere, even here in the GRANITE, for "Joe," below, — 'tis he.

name: Houser, Joseph
description: "Joe" came down from Canterbury to assist the Zoölogy Department in obtaining the highest scholastic standard on the campus. We must say "Joe" is determined, as he started work at the Marshall House only as a means of support while learning the game, for he strongly believes experience is the only teacher. Perhaps this is the reason why he is an honor student in Zoölogy. We are all sure of his success as a doctor, for he is so sympathetic.

name: Howell, Cecil Vernon
description: Dashing over from Rollinsford on that "bus," Howell spends most of his time as a traveling man. What he does the rest of the time besides studying and classes is our next disclosure. A bird's-eye view might disclose a rosy-cheeked farmerette leaning over a pasture bar dreaming of our Cecil. His place in life is not too tall or too short, but of an equal standing among all great men.

name: Hulbert, Alvin C.
description: "Hully" came to us from Northeastern with a reputation as an accountant and lived up to his advance notices by taking account of the campus in a week or two - results unfavorable, tho. And so he trekked over to Dover two or three times a week in search of new worlds to conquer. But he swears all women are detrimental to the progress of a struggling young C. P. A., so he has taken the vow of celibacy until he finishes college.

name: Hunt, Edward H.
description: "Ed" rolled into Durham in his little Ford racer, fresh from Exeter Academy and stopped long enough to register with the class of '29. The new college rule and the old age of his "tin-can" forced "Ed" to abandon the vehicle for higher scholastic and athletic attainments. As a guard on the freshman football team, he was unbeatable, and now he seeks honors as a puck-chaser on the varsity hockey squad. He's popular with the fair sex, and his inevitable smile and good nature make him popular with everyone else.

name: Hunt, Stanley Elwyn
description: The little boy with the curly hair; little but mighty. The co-eds don't know what they are missing, since "Stupe" does all his rushing outside the town. The towns around Durham all know this lad, and he sure dances a mean step. We think we know why "Stupe" spends so much time in the Chemistry Building, but we'll not tell.

name: Huntoon, Isabelle Madeline
description: That unmistakable laugh that "Izzy" brought with her to Durham, surely is contagious, and even the "quiet" proctor hasn't yet succeeded in getting her to control it. Does she know what time the Syracuse mails come in? She had that schedule memorized before she came back to school last September. Considering the fact that she has a great interest in Sociology we expect that she will find some use for it among the "murmuring pines and the hemlocks."

name: Jackson, John A.
description: "Jack" slipped quietly into his home town's namesake, and has been quiet ever since. Although few know him, those who do affirm he is a true friend. "Studying never does one any harm," "Jack" says, so thus he is gaining all he can while in this "north country." Economics and Accounting are just a waste of time to him.

name: Jennings, Robert O.
description: "R. Ote," as "Bob" is commonly known, loomed up as one of the most conspicuous members of the University Band during his freshman year, until the sorry time when the leader discovered he had no reed in his saxophone. However, don't allow this to stop you from aspiring to big things, "Bob." "R. Ote" has uncovered several "wonders" during his college career, due no doubt, to his prying nature. However, "Bob" proved one of the most valuable of the technical staff in the presentation of Mask and Dagger's, "Icebound" last fall.

name: Johnson, Charles Cleveland
description: If it means Actuating, Imitating, or Speculating, our most brilliant English student will sign on the dotted line. He loves them, and through them he makes his lovers. Sleeping in showers or "tenting" overnight in Smith Park to read his Milton by the light of dawn are Charlie's two desires. A true Doctor Jekyll and Mr. Hyde is this versatile youth. Charlie has migrated southward for the winter. Until his expected return in September, Isabelle will be the guiding spirit of Charles Cleveland Johnson, alias "Sophia and Chester," half man and half woman or—"the man in the Black Derby."

name: Johnson, James J.
description: "Jimmie" majors in just two courses—Chemistry and Mary—both interesting subjects. Having earned an M. S. at Dover "Biz.", "Jimmie" now treats the Co-eds in an attempt at his A.B. at U. N. H. We wish him success and we have no fear, because he has "IT" and is bound to succeed.

name: Johnson, Lloyd Charles
description: "Sock" is one of the products of Mont Vernon. No one knows much about this place or about "Sock." He wields a wicked dish at the Commons, and greets everyone with a pleasant smile. He has a "rep" of being a good scout and thus makes friends with all who come in contact with him. Best of luck, boy, and may there be lots of little "Socks" to carry on!

name: Jones, Walter L.
motto: Faint heart never won Fair Lady.
description: Here we have the midget of our class, he stands five feet three when barefooted, but size is not the only thing that counts. If you think so, try to keep pace with him in the Political Science Department. Dancing is his favorite sport and he sure excels in it.

name: Joyal, Raymond J.
description: "Ray" is a rare type of student - not easily found. We have been trying to locate him ever since his hat has been hung in Durham. "Studies are so easy," says "Ray." He is just another man in our class that found that out! Somersworth is so far away, "Ray," but may we come to the wedding?

name: Karabelas, Theodore L.
description: If "a friend in need is a friend indeed," then "Ted" is indeed a friend. "Ted" is more or less of an athlete of note and has done a great deal to promote athletics among the younger generation of Dover. It is surprising how, with all his extra activities, he can achieve such a high scholastic standing. If you wish to drive your blues away, page "Ted." In days to come we may live to see "Ted's" bust in the Hall of Fame.

name: Kelly, John T.
description: Somebody left the portals of Gate City open and so we have "Jackie" with us. When bigger and better lies are told, "Kelly" will tell them. He has never been seen without a smile on his countenance, which partly explains his popularity on the campus. When "Jackie" graduates, he plans to attend the University of Illinois - or Bryant and Stratton.

name: Kemp, Wyman Foster
description: "Beppo" thinks the "Pine Tree" State is where men are grown, but for all that he came to New Hampshire to be educated. Being a chemist, it's doubtful whether or not he'll ever be educated. "Bep's" girl lives in New York. That is a long way from here, but when he does see her, the former distance is forgotten and everything is O. K. - And how!

name: Kincaid, Norman
description: Anyone who has attended the home football games for the last two years has seen "Donk" running around waving his little red flag to the man on the score board. It looks very mystifying, but "Donk" says it is easy to do with a little practice. We notice that Norman held a position on the Rope Pull for two years. He may have used his pull to get his various positions as manager or possibly the job of waving the red flag, but we are very sure that he obtained them only through hard, persevering work.

name: Kirvan, Paul James
description: Paul received the cognomen of "handsome brute" from one of the professors last fall and since then has not had much time for the girls on campus, although he still makes his week-end trips to Portsmouth. During his first two years, however, Paul attended all the sorority dances and one in particular, he says, he can't forget. It wouldn't be an easy thing to do.

name: Knapton, Reginald F.
description: One sees little of "Reg," the "Henniker Evening Breeze," as most of his time is taken up with his Architecture course. You can always hear "Reg" before you can see him, for he possesses all the "blowing" characteristics of the breeze. However, the "blowing" is of a good enough kind, so we must forgive it. One thing we can't quite see, and that is how "Reg" spends so much time at Hampton Beach in the spring and gets any work done.

name: Knight, Raymond M.
description: Quiet and pleasant, pronounces "Ray" Knight. "Ray" is a big boy - physically and as a "techer." Something tells us that "Ray" has a big future ahead of him. We wish him our best and may he prosper as one of the "big" engineers.

name: Krabek, Wilfred B.
description: Wilfred is that scholarly boy with the "Peaches and Cream" complexion whom one finds most of the time at his bench in Conant Hall, chasing the elusive molecule. He is also one of the wearers of the "Pink" breeches, and we expect to hear much of him as an accomplished officer of "Uncle Sam's" army in a year or two.

name: Lane, Kenneth Stacy
clubs: First Sergeant R. O. T. C. (3)
description: "Prexy" rode down from the Capitol city to take first hand courses in the details of hash slinging. As a Commons waiter, he sure knows his stuff. In his spare time, he attends classes in the College of Technology. He has become a shark in his courses, and in whatever "Ken" undertakes he will be sure to succeed. That beautiful red hair makes him a favorite with the fairer sex. Too bad "Prexy," but we are not all born handsome.

name: Langford, Joseph Walton
description: Out of the crowded thoroughfares of East Candia to the fields of Durham, "Joe" came to be an aspiring member of the Class of 1929. He has decided to be an engineer, and aside from his leisure time, which is taken up by the fairer sex, he gleans the leaves of Math. and Physics toward an unexplained theory which he may sometime master. Good luck to you "Joe," and may you attain that end which you are now seeking.

name: Lawrence, Luther H.
description: "Luke" has finally settled down, with an Alpha Xi, of course. We've had it first hand that this is a sure thing, and for both concerned the best of luck is our wish. "Luke" is a splendid chap and a good room-mate, even if he does leave some of his clothes kicking around. The best of luck, "Luke" and we'll be glad to see you any time.

name: Learned, Robert Craig
description: In the fall of 1924, the "B. & M." brought one of its chief passengers to Durham. "Bob" said he came to college to accomplish three things: to become an engineer, to write fairy tales, and to make a hit with the co-eds. No one has yet surpassed him in story-telling, or attended more dances in a single year. He still maintains he is going to be an engineer, and if you don't believe it just ask him.

name: LeClaire, Pauline
description: "Polly" transferred to us from Lasell and along with her came those laughing brown eyes which we have learned express the cheerfulness and sincerity of her heart. Those of us who have been fortunate enough to make her acquaintance regret that she did not come to us sooner.

name: Ledoux, Eloise L.
description: Some time ago Eloise left her quiet domicile in Crescent Hall to become quiet proctor at the Chi Omega House. She now wishes that Crescent were still a girl's dormitory. There is often a far away look in "Ellie's" eyes and we wonder whether she is thinking of struggles on the battle fields or on athletic fields. "Ellie" likes variety, her "affaires de coeur" ranging from violinists to army officers. We think that Eloise has the future of a Galli-Curci!

name: Lindell, Ruth
motto: "All thoughts, all passions, all delights, Whatever stirs this mortal frame, All are but ministers of Love, And feed his sacred flame."
description: Ruth's career has been marked by spasmodic changes of interest. After sharking a few Spanish courses and Art she has turned her attention to Geography. Now she is listening for tremors from New Jersey from whence all mails lead to Durham.

name: Lothrop, Winston H.
description: "Bill" is just another Dover boy making good. He has traveled extensively, - being a commuter. Music is his profession, and studies, his hobby. He can certainly tickle the "ivories," and is the pianist in the "Beachland Blues Orchestra."

name: Lovell, Stewart F.
description: "Stewie" probably knows as many people on the campus as anyone, and why shouldn't he. A man in his business always does. "Stewie" should be awarded a prize as a rare barber, for unless you start it yourself he will not commence that barber's "gift of gab." "Stewie" is one of the star performers of the Glee Club.

name: Lucinski, Daniel Joseph
description: "Danny" is still reminded of the baseball trip to Bowdoin in the spring of his sophomore year and the time he dined at the "Chi Psi" (Chi Psi) House. He swears by the "Chi Psi" boys, and probably has plenty of silverware as souvenirs of the event. "Danny's" tan, acquired many summers ago, has failed to lose any of its ruddiness and has been the cause of many unpublished nicknames.

name: McIntyre, Harlan F.
description: A one-sided love affair must be heartbreaking. If you don't believe it, ask P — It is hard to believe that a man can be so hard-shelled as to be impervious to the loving glances of a bonnie lassie we could mention. But aside from women, "Mac" sure is of some account even though he is no 'count with the fairer sex.

name: McClenning, Edward
description: "Mac" first donned the harness with the class of 1928, but necessities called him away; hence, his appearance here. In the past he has been subjected to chronic attacks of "puppy love," but he claims "those days are gone forever." If determination is a measure of success, we expect high achievements of him in the future.

name: McCoey, Daniel F.
motto: Dan used to commute but the boxing team took up so much of his time that he decided to become a permanent fixture on this campus. He is a tower of strength on the fistic team and sure packs a mean wallop. Don't think, though, that he donates all of his time to fighting, for he does a lot of road work - in the evening. Whether it is a battle of wits or mitts, Dan is there and more than holds his own.

name: MacDonald, Raymond F.
motto: In the peace and quiet of Doc Slobin's classes, Mac has found his true place in Durham, and, being Scotch, he gets a kick out of close figuring. Mac has the same shy and retiring disposition as the Doc, but manages to hide it, except in bull sessions, where his observations, to say the least, are original, and not learned in DeMerritt.

name: McNab, Eric Lumsden
motto: The Pied Piper blew in from Scotland loaded with Scotch jokes and bag-pipes. He has now decided to improve on the works of nature by landscaping the garden. He has recently returned from Tennessee, where he materially helped to remedy some of the defects in the dairy industry. We'll all bank on Mac; he's a real Scotchman.

name: McNamara, Edward J.
motto: Hard-boiled Mac came down from the land of the Wah-Hoo-Wahs to the Wildcat campus. His athletic abilities came to the fore in basketball, and he was rewarded the coveted '29. Later he joined Hank Swasey's gang of assassins and became the roughest of the rough. Next to athletics, dodging the Dover Constabulary is his favorite pastime. Watch out, Mac; he who laughs last laughs best.

name: McShane, Helen Lavinia
description: Four years of college without cutting a class is the standard Helen sets for herself - and she'll make it, too. She belongs to the class of those who accomplish what they set out to do, and she doesn't leave her preps to be done on the way over in the bus. Helen is a true representative of U. N. H. as a scholar, athlete, and real friend.

name: McWeeney, Alice Frances
description: "Al" is our little colleen who specializes in dancing, pep, and heart-smashing (or perhaps her sisters might substitute "dish-smashing"). Her friends recommend a traffic cop to direct her daily hustle on the campus, for she always seems to be just leaving or just arriving between dates. However, she does find time to amuse us with her own inimitable brand of humor.

name: MacLaren, Pauline Rosalind
description: Just the sort of girl fair Helen must have been With golden hair and flashing, tender smile, Except that Grecian Helen was beloved by only men While Polly wins us all with her wiles. The sort of girl you dream about, - who doesn't oft come true Of whom you're awfully proud to say, "she came from N.H.U."

name: Mahoney, William A.
description: "Bill" is another of "Doc" Richards' protégés and he certainly knows his "Willie" Shakespeare. Aside from that, "Bill" is quite a frequenter of the informals and seems to have a preference for "tiny" Freshmen Co-eds. His head-waiter's job at the Commons certainly makes him fit with the Freshmen girls.

name: Mara, John J.
description: John Joseph Mara, a quiet, unobtrusive lad, is not a giant in size. His persistence in scholastic affairs bids fair to give humanity a conscientious physician in the years to come. "Chick" has been pursuing a pre-medical course, but we wonder why he doesn't love the campus enough to remain here week-ends. What about Commonwealth Avenue and St. Stephens Street, Boston, Mr. Mara? Explanations are in order. Perhaps you will get more acquainted with those sections of the Hub when you continue your study of human anatomy at Tufts or B. U. Medical school.

name: Mason, Walter S.
description: Realizing the state's need for poultrymen, this boy from Canobie Lake came to Durham to take up the rescue work and has succeeded in staying here three years. It is rumored that on long trips he carries his tomb-stone with him. However, if you want a thing done right, give it to "Walt," a student, a sport, and a prince of a friend to all.

name: Messenger, Marshall Edward
description: Being well read is an attainment that is admirable in any man. If in addition to this it can be said that he has travelled respectively, he must certainly be quite a personage. "Marty's" travels are not extensive, but occur very often when the lure of Boston approaches with the coming of the week-end. It does seem that "Marty" ought to give a little time to the co-eds, but he probably has his own reasons for not doing so.

name: Mooar, Willard E.
description: "Bill" came to college with the intention of becoming an engineer but later decided that the study of botany had a better future for him. "Bill" keeps away from the women while here at Durham but, as he goes home almost every week-end, we think that the big town of Hudson must have some attractions that we do not know about. If you see a dark-haired youth, with a cheery smile up around Nesmith Hall or the greenhouses, you will know that that's "Bill."

name: Moore, Everett B.
description: Now that "Dinty" is here for his college career, the girls in Keene are having a much needed rest, for as you must know, he's the sheik of the town. "Dinty" has a bad habit of being late for everything and so far we have been unable to obtain a reason for this, because he rarely says much. But when he speaks - Hearken, all ye people, for his are words of wisdom. He takes the Engineering course, but there are grave doubts of his ever achieving a marked success in this field. His beautiful tenor voice is to be heard any day at the Lambda Chi House. Poor Lambda Chis!

name: Morrissette, Leon
description: "Morry" is one of these quiet unassuming fellows who apparently doesn't know that New Hampshire is a co-educational school, but on week-ends he is one of the bright lights of "Farming-town" where the daughters of the chief residents flock to him like flies to sorghum molasses. "Morry" has squared "pies" in the "Tech" department and cubed pies at Jack Grant's for three years. We are sure that "Morry" will successfully outwit the cruel business world as he has the professors at the University and the fellows of Farming-town.

name: Morton, Paul Fillmore
description: Paul came to us in his freshman year, a quiet, scholarly youth from the Pine Tree State. We are sorry to relate that during the last two years, owing to his co-educational duties, his scholarship has suffered to a certain extent. We also have learned that he is thinking of attending religious school next summer. Anyway, Paul, you may be sure that whatever you do, we wish you the best of success.

name: Moynihan, Claire
description: We came to the realization that college education was doing its duty to "Buttercup" when she came back from the flood district and informed us that her home town was "all wet." The one thing about college which she relishes above all else is leaving it week-ends. She is a reticent damsel on such subjects, however, and we are merely left to speculation concerning the object of these trips.

name: Murdock, Elizabeth L.
description: She shines as a ouija board artist and especially tobogganing as her neighbors across the way can affirm. Her culinary ability came well to the foreground this past summer, although her carelessness in cooking a chicken nearly ruined her reputation. We're always aware of "Liz'" cheerful smile about the campus, and her powerful boot on the soccer field.

name: Neal, Albert R.
description: "Al" certainly doesn't say much. When he does, however, it is well to listen, because a lot of thinking has been going on behind that curtain of silence. Many men come to college to study, but not so many actually do so. Some way or other "Al" finds time to do his very well, and is very sure to turn in marks that anyone would be glad to show the folks. As well as studies, "Al" does creditably in athletics, and is a mighty fine fellow to know.

name: Nelson, Wallace S.
description: This big, blonde boy came over from the "City by the Sea" with a lingo which, we think, he must have picked up from his long association with sea-faring men. He goes big with the fairer sex, having been out with at least half of the co-eds on the campus. His line and ready wit make him a most agreeable person with whom to associate. Although he has never been known to hurt himself studying, he has always managed to come through somehow.

name: Nichols, John Ballou
clubs: Regimental Sergeant-Major R. O. T. C. (2)
description: "Jock" is one of those good-natured unassuming boys. He does, however, find time to break away from his studies now and then to give some snappy brunette a good time — "No blondes for me," says "Jock." "Jock" will not admit that he has high ideas, but nevertheless he says that he is going to buy an aeroplane when he grows up, if he doesn't get killed on his motorcycle first.

name: Nichols, Maurice Seymour
description: "Nick" came to the University from Penacook to become a lawyer. They say a lawyer must have a good line, and he surely possesses this characteristic. No one has ever got the best of him in an argument, that being his favorite pastime. He refutes the theory that the little men do not amount to anything. "Nick" is often down, but never out. He always comes up with a smile, and his pleasing personality and ever ready wit place him on top. Whatever he sets out to do, he finishes and we feel that success will be his only end.

name: Nodes, Norbert C.
description: Introducing "K. O." Nodes from "Bergenfield." "K. O." is there with his mitts, and is also the "terror of the Commons." His weak point is "women," but we are sure all the Manchester girls appreciate "God's gift." Recently "K. O." has developed such a mania for music that "Ma" Leighton has made him a "victrola-man." Seriously though, we would miss our little bit of Irish wit if he were not here. "K. O." is a go-getter, so we feel assured of his success in future life.

name: Norris, Ruth G.
description: Ruth has tried other schools before ours, but we are hoping that she will make this her Alma Mater. Quiet and unassuming, she has won all our hearts by her cheerful manner, and unselfish ways. Ruth has a particular weakness for "cousins," but we forgive it all when she smiles at us in that bewitching way.

name: Arnold, Noyes
description: "Noisey," our star half-miler, is quite a boy in several ways - women - wine - song. However, he has made good working for "Ma" Leighton and can do his stuff pretty well under any influence. He says he has given up hope of ever being anything but a general utility man but we expect him to get his share of the good things and honors.

name: Gertrude, Elizabeth Nye
description: You may say in the words of Sir James Matthew Barrie that "Gertie" is "quiet and unassuming," but you can't get around the fact that she is also an all around girl. We've seen her do some pieces of acting that would turn many a professional green with envy, and as for sharking her English courses, well - ask "Dad" - he knows.

name: Esther, Elizabeth Otis
description: Why do short people wear high heels? If Esther can be made to tell, that problem will be solved correctly. However, try to make her tell you! When you see her toss that straight brown hair, or when you hear a contagious giggle down the street, you'll know that there's a mystery to it. But when Esther speaks in her best school-marm-ish voice, watch out for the brass tacks of reality!

name: Isabelle, Paige
description: "Izzy" is a demon to all her foes in athletics, and a shark in all her courses. Psi Lambda recognizes her ability in Home Ec., and the bleachers know her form on the cinder track of gala days, and perhaps on a few evenings also, eh, "Izzy?"

name: Pasquale, Paolino
motto: Give the ball to Pat, hollers the crowd, when the Blue and White needs a few yards.
description: Sometimes a student, but always a friend, our Pat knows them all - yes, girls, too, - and why not? Look him over, girls. He's got IT.

name: Sylvester, M. Parshley
motto: Doc blew into Durham all set for studying, but something side-tracked him. We think it was the female of the species.
description: He makes numerous trips to the home town, for some unknown reason. Doc is in the Army also. He sure made a hit at camp last summer after coming in from his week-end parties, especially when he found his bunk missing, but he always came out on top. His stories were enjoyed by all. He is now hitting the books and we're sure he will make good in the old battle of life.

name: Cynthia, Madalyn Patten
motto: The gods were kind, and from Lasell sent us Pat, for which we offer unlimited libations.
description: Charming, distinctive, perpetually enshrouded in an enchanting air of refinement, she has without effort captured all hearts - male or female. She is the kind of a girl every mother wishes her daughter to be - briefly, she is a success.

name: Charlotte, M. Pearl
motto: Good things come in small packages.
description: Little but peppy, that's Pete! She certainly loves to dance and how she can! We have watched Pete whirl around the floor at all informals and formals for three years and we feel competent to judge.

name: Peaslee, Charlotte Lucy
motto: She speaketh not and yet there lies a conversation in her eyes.
description: "Charlie's" eyes are always giving away her secrets. When you see that mischievous twinkle, beware! They say she's even been guilty of skipping dates. We wonder if her heart ever turns West. When in doubt watch her eyes!
