name: Perkins, John Fremont
description: "Johnny" comes from Meredith, where men are men and women count, at least in "Johnnie's" young life. "Perk" is taking engineering and is a little doubtful. However, by his perseverance he is still with us. To get a little of the practical side of life, "Johnny" took a year off and went to Florida. That is what caused the big boom down there. It's too bad for Florida that he came back to U. N. H.

name: Phelps, Ruth Beveridge
motto: Did you see those eyes? Oh! "Phelpsie" where did you get them?
description: Isn't it nice that you are not under dormitory rules? If you ever learn to drive that car be sure there is a "Jack" in the tool box. "Phelpsie" is the kind of a girl you can't forget, and she will not let you forget the A. T. O.'s. She says her summers are interesting, but she wishes she could work in a bank.

name: Pinney, Warren E.
clubs: Platoon Sergeant R. O. T. C. (3)
description: What Ho! And this is no unsophisticated youth. No! No! He hails from the village of Springfield, where women are forbidden and men don't care. It is rumored that "Money-mad Pinney" imported his girl for the Manchester game and spent over three ($3.00) dollars for the week-end. However, "Pinney's" ability as a shekel-gatherer foretells of great riches to come.

name: Pitcher, Ruth Emery
motto:
All of us must surely know
The girl that plays, and learns to sew,
The girl that wears a certain pin
That signifies she's never in.
From this to that, you see her tear,
Such versatility's most rare.

name: Potter, Dean Spaulding
clubs: Platoon Sergeant R. O. T. C. (3)
description: Seeing a big letter advertisement in the paper, Dean took the dog team and from Acworth he journeyed to Durham to become an engineer. At the Commons he has mastered the art of hash slinging. During the winter months he spends his time pushing the skis. He is rather quiet and says the least of his worries is the fairer sex. But why should Dean and his flivver spend so much time away from Durham? That remains unexplained.

name: Prew, Gyneth
clubs: Alpha Omicron Pi at Jackson College
description: "Gynie" is conclusive proof that gentlemen do prefer 'em. But don't let her curls or her big eyes fool you into thinking she doesn't know what it is all about! She makes frequent trips to Boston - either for piano lessons or dental treatment, no doubt. Anyhow she is one of the few fortunates who knows everybody, goes everywhere, and sees everything.

name: Putnam, Lawrence Sargent
description: Ding! Bang! What was that? Oh, it was "Put," smiling cashier at the Commons, putting some students' money in the cash register. In his three years here we have seen him climb from waiter to cashier. Now we are waiting for some movie scout to request him to take Rudolph Valentino's place at Hollywood. The side boards sure will help him win this goal.

name: Rabinovitz, Archie
motto: And the class of 1929 will be remembered in days to come because they were the classmates of Archie, the poet, philosopher, psychologist, mathematician, author extraordinaire and what have you. We who have known him will never forget him. As we sit before that fireplace, thinking of the days that passed all too soon, we will still remember him, sloppily dressed, walking along the corridors of the age-worn Barracks, muttering, 'And the memories linger yet, There are — we can't forget.'

name: Raymond, Mary
description: Although Mary didn't come to us until this fall, her perpetual smile and ever ready "Hello" have won for her innumerable friends. Her attentions, however, are focussed upon one of the opposite sex — a blond freshman cheer leader. We trust that these attentions will not interfere with her one great aim — to teach Latin, when graduated from U. N. H.!

name: Record, Hattie F.
description: "Tine" is a woman of ideas — IDEAS, and she isn't a bit bashful about expounding them either. But where would some of us be without her very sane reasoning? Her very quietness and reserve at times make us wonder just where her thoughts may be — and perhaps how serious? So here's to "Tine" — we like her even when she's laughing at us!

name: Reed, Frank Dudley
motto: Little we see in nature that is ours.
description: Generous, even in the Commons; industrious, even in a fraternity room; pleasant, even during final week; considerate, even when very busy; popular, even with our co-eds; always a friend. Meet "Frankie."

name: Reed, Herbert Briggs
motto: Nobody loves a fat man, huh?
description: Guess the fellow that said that didn't know "Punkin'" and didn't know he traveled all the way to Keene every week to see his "mother." She loves him. "Punkin'" went out for football two years. He didn't succeed in making his letter, but who could play football and make his letter after playing two full games two days in succession on his home field? Ask anyone that knows and they will tell you that a more likeable chap never graced this campus. Why, of course, his Dad's a minister! Who'd uh thunk it?

name: Reed, Gordon F.
description: "Pep" has been a member of the Glee Club for the past three years and he surely has developed a liquid voice. Beware ye who would cross his path and have the curse of God brought down upon your unfortunate head! "Pep" generally drives somebody's car home after "exams" every term.

name: Reinhart, Alvin R.
description: "Al" is one of the Alpha Xi Delta pledges from the Theta U. House. But, knowing "Polly," one doesn't blame "Al" at all. He finds it rather hard not to be able to go over to see her during the training season, but that is the drawback of being an athlete. "Al" plans to be an architect, and we'll wager he'll make a good one. If he can use a pencil the way he can a hockey stick or a baseball, there will be no question about it.

name: Rice, John C.
description: Don't let this man fool you with his quiet appearance 'cause John may be found commuting to Dover or Newmarket any of these balmy "nights." John is quite a man with his lines. In architecture he is a member of the honorary architecture society; in football he was always on a bee line for the goal; in track he toed the white chalk line, throwing the discus and javelin. He has won a host of friends since he came to us from Tilton.

name: Richardson, Homer Huntress
description: "Radio Rich" rides over from Dover each day on the Commuters' bus just to see how many more one-hundreds his professors in the Technology Department have given him. Just where he gets his brains and ability to talk we are at a loss to explain, though we fear there is a feminine weakness guiding him. How about it "Rich?" Ask him how he worked last night with his radio transmitter and in five minutes you will know more about radio than Marconi.

name: Robinson, David
description: If a "wheezer's" club is ever organized there is no doubt but what "Robbie" will be the first president, "ex-officio" or otherwise. But for all his wheezing he is certainly a jack-of-all trades. We hear of certain reports... but we hope that he never gets bumped off with a rolling pin.

name: Rourke, Eugene
description: "Gene" leads the easy life of a commuter but nevertheless he sharks all his history courses. His interests are not centered around this hazardous task though. We wonder why he goes to Boston so often. "Gene" is a good pal and we wish him the best of luck.

name: Roy, Joseph Sandy
description: "Sandy" comes from Amesbury, Mass., but in the three years he has been here he has somewhat outlived that drawback, and has shone as one of the stars with Coach Cowell's cohorts. He has never been seen with a co-ed, but some say that his most outstanding work on the gridiron was performed when a certain little miss from Mass. was in the bleachers. He holds an honorable membership in the L. W. V.'s, and is popular not only with his classmates but also with his profs.

name: Sacco, Victor
motto: Big Things Come in Little Packages
description: "Vic," our Class midget, has become a big man among us. "Vic" came from Portsmouth with a big smile and a pleasing personality which won for him a host of friends. He has shown us by his work in the Intramurals that the manly art of self defense is much to his liking. This care-free, happy-go-lucky fellow left us at the end of the fall term our junior year and he sure is missed.

name: Sargent, Robert Everett
description: In the fall of 1925 "Bob" came from the "Paper city" with hopes of becoming an engineer. Since then he has decided to become an Arts and Grafter. He still holds the first thought in store for he owns a modern flivver, which he rattles to Franklin every week end. When he is not entertaining the co-eds, he turns his attention to the depths of Economics and Accounting. "Bob" has a pleasing personality and we all like him. Watch your step, "Bob," they fall hard.

name: Schiller, Robert W.
description: Ever since a certain lady in Lynn saw our fair "Bob" in an officer's uniform it has been impossible for us to get the idea out of his head that he is to be a second Foch. But in spite of all his faults and a spacious awning over his lunchcart, "Bob" sure has got "it," and we can't take "it" away from him because "Issy" has "it" too.

name: Scripture, Paul Newton
motto: If you let the grass grow under your feet, it might as well grow over your head.
description: Did you ever hear about "Scrip's" adventures in Nashua? Just mention the Mil. Art. hike to him and watch the grin appear. At least for him the Mil. Art. hike was a success, for "Scrip" now believes in Co-eds even if he hasn't given them much time.

name: Seaver, Frank Raymond
description: We don't see much of Frank's social side here at school, but we understand that he's right there just the same. He loses his pin occasionally, but he usually gets it back. What we'd like to know is, how does he get it back? That's all right Frank, bring the girls around once in a while and let the fellows see them.

name: Seavey, Gordon B.
description: Gordon may blow a trumpet in the band, but as for blowing his own horn, he leaves that to others. Last year at B. U., Gordon won every honor available, being president of his class, member of the Student Council, etc. Now he has started to do the same here, and the least we can say is that he will come through.

name: Sewell, Charles A.
description: Here is "Charlie," this curly-haired commuter from Dover. He's to be one of our future "math profs." We all know that Mathematics and Chemistry don't give him indigestion. Some night he expects to drop a bag of gum drops from the top of the new chimney, and then calculate how long it takes for each gum drop to reach "terra firma." When "Charlie" is not "sharking the math course" we find him handing out bargains in one of Dover's leading department stores.

name: Shedd, Bernard
description: "Tiny-top" hails from West Manchester - within the shadow of the gas-house. He has many activities, but as a chemist he is a decided success. He hibernates in "Chem. Lab." six days a week, and just for diversion, works in "Chem. Lab." the seventh. His greatest and fondest wish is to become Mayor of Belmont, Mass. That's where his "heart" is! "Bernie" toots a mean sax, and the other three original L. W. V.'s are proud of him. We wonder what there is about Portsmouth that frightens him so; he doesn't dare go near there without first donning his running suit.

name: Silvia, Francis John
description: After three years of football, Frank finally brought his light from under the bushel and startled the campus with some spectacular broken field running. He may have received the practice from running up and down the scale with the University Trio, but that doesn't matter. He made good, and now sports an "N.H."

name: Sirakis, Nicholas J.
description: "Nick" claims he is a junior. "Dad" claims he is a sophomore. We'll stick with "Nick" and so the vote is two to one against "Dad." Just another engineer, but he concentrates his attention to the ways and means of transportation to and from Somersworth, where he is hailed as "It's Willie Hoppe." Despite the fact that he is seldom seen on the campus, he makes his presence known in bridge and in basketball.

name: Slack, Rita
description: Rita is a very conscientious young lady when it is a question of quiet-proctoring. She won't even give her room-mates a break! But when she receives invitations from a certain young "Ladd" she is not so conscientious, for she never refuses. We wonder if the car that Rita drives around so much has a Maine license on it "just to be different?"

name: Smith, Addison L.
description: "Smitty" is musical - believe it or not. He can blow his horns and saw his fiddle. The band is centered around him. He came to us from B. U. where one year of night life was almost too much for the boy from Woodsville. Every Saturday night one can locate him in a telephone booth calling up the garage about how soon his "Lizzie" will be ready. He always gets there (to the dance) with the rest of the orchestra, all tuned up and waiting for the leader.

name: Smith, Charles Mumier
motto: What a man he turned out to be!
description: "Charlie" came to us as a Junior, having completed two years at Antioch College in Ohio. Why he left Antioch is unknown - unknown at least to most of us, but some say that it was too far from Laconia. We wonder why? He wasn't homesick; we know that. Of course there is always a - but that's all over now. Charles knocks all his courses for a loop. He spends his spare time telling the farmers around Laconia how to keep the milk pail away from the pump.

name: Smith, Edward
description: "Smithy" is not as well known as he might be for two reasons. He has spent most of his time nursing the flowers in the University greenhouse, and he is an architect. That is, he is taking a course in architecture. However, all who know him certainly like him and know that whether it's nursing flowers or nursing - well, never mind, "Smithy" is O. K., what we mean.

name: Smith, Esther Hervey
motto: Here's to Esther - oh so good! She'd never do What the rest of us would.
description: The girls all appreciate Esther's ability as a hair dresser before a dance - and can she dance? Say, she knows all the latest steps!

name: Smith, Frederick H.
motto: Behold the child by Nature's kindly law - pleased with a rattle, tickled with a straw.
clubs: Winner 1st Prize, Manual of Arms Competition (1)
description: Fred, "Lindy" Smith has had a flighty journey during his three year stay with us. Fred, likeable and popular, will be 1929's candidate to fill the shoes of Lindbergh in the future. An amusing thing happened to Fred the other night. Fred has been in the habit of experimenting with various members of a club housed in a frame building. When he called the other evening, one of his rejected loves answered the bell, smiled sweetly, and said, "Which one tonight, Fred?"

name: Smith, Richard Emery
description: "Em" is small but Oh My! He takes a great delight in doing fantastic solo dances, being an expert in the execution of Charleston and Oriental dance rhythms. Yet we feel that some co-ed is missing something by not having "Em" as her partner at a college dance. His collegiate career has been unblemished by feminine attractions, but these frequent trips home - there's the mystery.

name: Smith, Rowland H.
clubs: Advertising Manager of the New Hampshire (2)
Advertising Manager of the Golden Bull (3)
Business Manager of the Freshman Handbook (2)
description: Rowland Howard Smith - the mere mention of his name should be enough, but there is a story which all should know. Briefly, "Rollie" is a philosophical business man, and after much deep thought and investigation he has decided to get his sheepskin from U. N. H., after looking over Dummer, Phillips Andover, Newton High and Williams College. "Rollie" has been managing something ever since he decided to settle, and yet at the same time he is a single man with but a single thought - Derrum.

name: Smith, Ruth M.
description: Who cooks when the cook is gone? Who straightens all the "naughty" problems which seem so unconquerable to those outside the realm of "Home Ec"? Finally, who's the "family fix-it"? "Ruthie," none other. She looks up smiling even from diving, at which we all admit she's a "shark." But we have cause to weep - she has forsaken household duties for the brush and pallet.

name: Smith, Wilmot Haven
description: "Smitty" left us for a year, but he's a bear for punishment, - he's back and expects to graduate. Asked what course he was to graduate in, "Smitty" replied that he expected to graduate in the course of time. When he left Plymouth, it was reported that the Normal School had disbanded, but "Smitty" denies it and avows allegiance to the one and only. He also makes frequent sojourns to Boston - supposedly on business.

name: Snodgrass, Robert L.
motto: "Snoddy," alias "Pecky," is in accordance with the accepted phrase "Another Swede from the North," for who could deny Scandinavian ancestry with such hair? But "Snoddy" affirms that the color of his hair is no criterion. He may be right about the ancestry, but he can't deny other qualities possessed by red-heads. However, when it comes to choosing a good sport and willing worker one can't go wrong on picking "Snoddy" with his entertaining whistle.

name: Somers, Arthur Ladd
motto: Whenever you want to know who are the new girls on the campus, ask "Art." We hear that he has them all indexed in his little book. "Art" is also a debater. Probably he is as well fitted for that line as anybody on campus. Among the several reasons we'll have for remembering "Art" in after years will be his wonderful acting in the leading role of "Icebound."

name: Soper, Carolyn E.
motto: Although "Carol" is a transfer from Mass. Aggie, she has made many acquaintances. The third floor crowd of Congreve realizes her ability as a hostess - especially after the laundry case arrives! Who hasn't sampled her cookies and brownies? Who cares to diet with such goodies around? Not "Carol!"

name: Spencer, Harold C.
motto: "Tubby" is the ray of sunshine from Northwood. He is one of those fellows who likes New Hampshire so well that he plans to stay here six or seven years. "Tubby" is a shark in two things - pool and cribbage. We are informed also that he was "basket tennis" champion of Ballard Hall during his freshman year. If everyone had "Tubby's" pleasant disposition, this world would be a remarkable place. We are sure he has a great future.

name: Spinney, Alice
motto: Spinney may be a blonde, but she isn't light-headed, for she proves her mental capacity when she adopts argumentation as her favorite indoor sport. Whether it is winning tennis championships, playing hockey, or sharking all her courses, Spinney is right there. We're proud of our Spin, and we doff our hats to her as an all-round girl, athlete and scholar.

name: Spinney, Wesley William
motto: The Woodsman came out of the north country, set his compass, and headed for Durham. In his hand he carried a small stick. The first place he stopped was Dean Taylor's office. 'What kind of wood is this?' he said. The Dean handed him a bunch of roll cards and showed him the pages in the catalogue on forestry. Wes decided then and there that he would become a forester.

name: Sprague, Clarence E.
motto: After trying a sailor's life Spragie decided that he could get better meals elsewhere, so he signed up for the Advanced Mil. Art. and started eating at the Commons. By the number of letters he writes we conclude that he lived up to the old saying about a sailor having a girl in every port.

name: Sprague, Louise
motto: Behold Squee—protégé of the Dean of Women, embryo Phi Bete, successor to George Washington. Her ability matches her hair, but her temper—doesn't! Squee particularly enjoys horseback riding and is always accompanied by a broncho Buster. Squee's record is sufficient proof of her versatility; if you want anything done, Squee will do it.

name: Stark, Lewis Morgrage
description: And it came to pass that Lewis came to ornament our campus with his great fame, and to win his way into his classmates' hearts by the way of the "ivories." Whether he follows the career of Paderewski or of his ancestors of the revolution he is sure to be a "Whiz Bang" success. But look out Lewis, the world is a large and woolly place.

name: Starke, Robert J.
clubs: Member of Board of Directors, GRANITE (3)
description: "Red," or "Oscar" as he is often called, is perhaps the purest member of the class. His heart is pure gold, his soul is pure white, his hair is pure copper, and his head is — oh, he is pure all right. He rates very well with a certain sorority which often finds him present at dinner. Sometimes when this occurs they have actually invited him. He is president of our class and, as this implies, we like him rather well. That includes the masculine half of the class, too.

name: Stevens, Jesse John
description: In the fall of 1925, "Steve" jumped astride a stick of pulp-wood, pushed himself into the middle of the stream, floated out of the north woods and decided to come to college. He divides his time between the co-eds, the Commons and the class rooms. "Steve's" smooth line has caught many a fair one off balance. He always has a smile whenever you see him and we place great faith in him as a master of history in the future.

name: Stewart, Lucy Catharine
description: Like the Sphinx of Egypt "Lucy Catharine" hasn't much to say, but she looks a great deal. Her goings and comings are very much a mystery, but people have a right to their own opinions. She claims she has had a great past, but we still have hopes for her future. She wishes the fact that she is a "Home Ec-er" be kept secret, but many a good woman has missed her calling.

name: Stocker, William A.
description: After two years of heavy studying at Tilton School, "Bill" decided to enter New Hampshire in quest of an Architectural Education. He works diligently at his drawing but never signs up for courses that come before ten o'clock in the morning. Last summer "Bill" met something pretty nice from Rhode Island, and he says he would transfer to Rhode Island State if he could find a course to take down there. However, if everyone here used the U. S. Mail as much as "Bill" does our taxes would go up.

name: Sumner, George Carter
description: George came to college to show them how to do things. He started off with a bang - but the poster fight found him with a big lip. George is a sheik with the women - he has been on the point of speaking to one little girl on the campus ever since he came here. Hurry up, George, you only have a year left. "Soup's" quiet temperament is broken by witty and humorous remarks which make him a favorite in the famous "bull" sessions.

name: Tahaney, John William
description: Elinor Glynn once spent the summer in Beverly. It was there that she met "Will." He was directing traffic for his father at the entrance to their ancestral mansion. When the summer was over Elinor returned home to write "It." "Officer" Tahaney transferred from Boston College last year, and soon won the friendship of all who met him. His ability as a basketball player has been demonstrated in Inter-fraternity games. The least we can say of "Will" is that what was B. C.'s loss is our gain.

name: Taylor, Julia Alice
description: "Dode" spends most of her time down by the old mill stream, where she may often be seen in a bright red canoe. We know very little about her, except that she is very much interested in the Kappas, that she has a very pronounced interest in a certain military school, and that she is an all-round good sport. We would like to know how she got her drag with the faculty and where she goes after fraternity meetings.

name: Tetley, Ronald E.
description: His love is skating and his weakness, women, but we can hardly blame him. "Tet" can be seen showing the heels of his steel-runnered shoes to his opponents during the winter sports season, but as the winter goes by and the summer days begin to make themselves conspicuous on the campus, "Tet" is seen steadily hitting the road to Dover. We all wonder — why?

name: Theall, John W. Jr.
description: "Kid" blew in from West Side High, Manchester, and has been blowing ever since. In his sophomore year, he started stepping out, but being disappointed in love, he turned his attention to Dover and Berwick. He spends a lot of time telling the "Frosh" where to get off, and how great he used to be back in his prime.

name: Thompson, Roger R.
description: From Somersworth he comes, they say, To B. A. he once did go, And now for old N. H. he strives, And 'Lisbeth, by the way. His courses one and all be sharks, O'er business he hopes to lord. Before the gym each day he parks His one-lung open Ford.

name: Thompson, Ruth E.
motto: "A violet by a mossy stone"
description: Quiet and unassuming — that's Ruth! She's "Johnnie on the spot" for the Zoology department, and she sharks all of her History courses. She's a good friend and a true one. She may be quiet, but there's always a twinkle in her brown eyes. Ruth is a proof that it's quality and not quantity that counts.

name: Tilc, Edward F.
description: "Eddie" is a quiet lad who renews that old saying, "much ado about nothing." As a student he is good, but we understand that when it comes to landscape gardening he's a "wow," and all Groveton hopes some day to benefit from this art. Psycho-analysis is another of "Eddie's" specialties but we fear he has a bit of an inferiority complex when it comes to women. Go to it, "Eddie boy"!

name: Timmins, Mary Elizabeth
description: "Parle-t-elle français?" Ask Cercle Français. Does she do anything else? We'll say! Student par excellence, athlete - if anything on campus needs doing, you may be sure "Tim" can do it. As for off campus, - well, there you'll find her stalling. Her daily trek from Newmarket Road is her training field, and as holder of the girls' high jump record, she can clear all obstacles.

name: Tobey, Lester B.
description: Between Forestry, Managership, North Hampton, and Ballard Hall, "Tobe" is kept well occupied. One would think that he is a worldly "man of affairs," but "Les" has only one "affair" of importance. Even in the dazzling light of this great enterprise, "Tobey" manages, through almost super-human efforts, to cast an occasional glance at his studies. This slight glance is sufficient, however, to insure an average of eighty-five or more. We sometimes wonder, "Les," how you do it. We'd like to be able to use our brains as proficiently as you do. Oh! Another thing! we're still waiting for the cigars, "Tobe."

name: Tompkins, Lois U.
clubs: Pi Beta Phi, University of Vermont
description: Pi Beta Phi, U. V. M., U. N. H. via Hanover, York Beach, Boston, week-ends, classes, Essex, Chrysler, Fords, Burlington and mud. "I'm sorry, but I'm not going to be here this week-end." "Long distance call for Lois from Salem." "I didn't mean to cut you, I didn't see you." Add a large quantity of personality, good looks and brains, and you get - Lois.

name: Toone, Malcolm P.
motto: A howling west-wind blew 'Zeke' into town, and it must have been contagious, because he has been howling in the Glee Club and the Church Choir ever since. When he is not doing that, he is using his violin to saw off 'Toones,' and boy! How he can saw 'em! No recital or dramatic representation is complete without 'Zeke' and his fiddle.

name: Tuttle, George I.
motto: Designated by all to be the Premier C. E. of the world are the plans we have laid out for 'Tut.' Quiet, refined and reserved is the boy from 'way down Maine.' If his aspirations are equalled by his achievements, '29 wishes him luck, and he need have no fear for his success.

name: Twombly, Gertrude
motto: 'Gert' brought those red lips and that easy-going disposition from the seclusion of Wheaton to the wide open spaces of New Hampshire. One wouldn't think her a woman of mystery but she has a hidden talent - just put a violin in her hands! 'Gert' maintains that she is a man-hater, but still uniforms have that certain fascination! And we don't mean R. O. T. C.!

name: Vatcher, George
motto: 'Vatch' is another one of those boys vainly trying to become an engineer. Some of the engineers will remember him as the man who tried to draw a couple of thousand amperes out of a bus bar. Still others may remember him as the person who shot a perfectly good basket for Hetzel, while vainly trying to defend the honor of Fairchild. 'Vatch' says his chief college pleasures are playing tennis, going to the movies, and bowling (in Dover).

name: Ross, Vilardo
motto: To 'Hades' with the cold and then it will be warm.
description: Two years at William and Mary makes "Sal" a newcomer to us. He is hard to get acquainted with, but a lasting friend to the ones he likes. He hasn't had a chance to demonstrate his basketball ability but he is quite clever at the game, as any "W and M" student will tell you. One of his weaknesses isn't women, thus we have a sane man among us.

name: Stanley, Vogel
motto: The little man behind the 'big' wheel and a good case for the Sociology department, is none other than our smiling 'Stan.'
description: Week-ends and the good ol' bus seem to be the main diet on "Sid's" bill of fare. We often wonder how he does it and what he does. Oh, you "cozy" engineer! "Sid" considers Civil Engineering as a side line. We know that he is quite "civil" but how about the rest? Good luck to our most famous "pretzel" eater!

name: Carl, E. Walker
motto: Johnnie Walker hails from old Contoocook - way up in the hills.
description: Many have seen him, but few really know him. He is as solitary as the hills whence he comes. Nevertheless, he sure knows his stuff. When any of the Foresters get stuck on a problem the question is: "Where's 'Johnnie' Walker? He'll know." And he does. In his freshman year he gained fame as a cross country runner. At the Forestry summer camp, because of his ability to cover a large amount of ground in a short time, he was made camp mailman. He is known by the people of Canaan as the mountain "go-it" because of the way he scampers over the hills.

name: James, Gray Walls
motto: Jimmy has had some hard bumps while in college but nobody can stop that ringing laugh of his.
description: No wonder he weighs two hundred. "Jimmy" never had to take Mil. Art. because the doctors said he was too heavy on his feet. We never think so when we see him whirling around the little gym at informals.

name: Weber, William F.
description: Weber - the man nobody knows much about! "Bill" goes to college from Monday until Friday, then no one knows what he does. He has never to our knowledge favored a co-ed with even a smile, but we think there must be some favorite somewhere or else why does he go away every Friday? There must be some magnetic attraction over in Manchester somewhere. Best of luck, "Bill."

name: Wendelin, Carl G.
clubs: First Sergeant, R. O. T. C.
description: Besides being able to cope with a course in the College of Technology, "Wendy" manages to include quite a bit of social activity in his curriculum. During the winter he plays varsity hockey to take up any extra time he might have. In his freshman year he made his numerals in football and has put up some good fights in the intramural boxing ring. "Wendy" is always ready to have something said to him because he can come back with better than is given him.

name: Wendell, John
description: John is good, speedy, and fair. For example, in one of his recent speed demonstrations, for the benefit of the other contestant he slid the last hundred feet on his stomach, still winning the race. John is a clean-cutting skater and he follows the same tactics in his school life. He is sure to make good in life if he uses the tools of his profession as cleverly as he has his slide-rule and skates during his school life.

name: West, Marjorie M.
clubs: Y. W. C. A. Nominating Committee (1)
description: When "Margie" is around we all know it, for who hasn't heard her rollicking laughter and gay calls? Although she doesn't blow her own horn, we must mention that as a saxophone player she surely excels. Sports in particular are "Margie's" favorites. Her unfailing good nature makes her one of the most popular girls of '29.

name: Wetherbee, Frederick M.
description: Here, ladies, we have "Bobo." He is none other than the one and only "Bobo," leader of the famous "Bobonians," the orchestra in which Paul Whiteman got his start. In the summer time this team of music mongers make light the feet of millions, when they rip off some heart-rending song such as "Where Is My Wandering Boy Tonight." "Bobo" has no use for women, - any more. In an interview with an Etude reporter he said, "I do not choose to run."

name: Wettergreen, John A.
description: When "Winto" came up here from Malden he was immediately spotted as one of these city slickers we hear so much about. "Winto" stepped into a regular berth on the football team and has been the mainstay of the line ever since. Clean sportsmanship and fair play characterize him in everything he undertakes. The weekly paper that he receives from a Malden Church will keep him from going "wrong" and help him to follow the "Wright" path.

name: Wiggin, Howard Melvin
description: Although a man of mystery to many, "Wig" to the few is a great farmer and, from the way he attends to his duties now, we can surely say that he will be a success in truck-gardening. He has made plans to return to his native land, Stratham, where he will begin the raising of vegetables. He believes in large scale production, but we hope he will not carry a large turnip wherever he goes.

name: Willey, Henry A.
description: "Baldy's" ambition has always been to govern his home town in the capacity of mayor. And who has a better right than Durham's own "Baldy"? Born and reared in this great metropolis, he has represented Durham unofficially, unfailingly, and we might say, unrepentingly, at Salisbury and Hampton beaches, as well as at other fashionable summer resorts on the Atlantic seaboard. He is a confirmed woman hater, but we attribute this latter virtue to the fact that he has been forced to live among the co-eds here all his life. Nevertheless, "Baldy" pulls down the grades and is making a name for himself.

name: Woodman, Louise
description: Why "X" would be "Woodie's" favorite haunt is a problem hard to solve. Louise left B. U. to join our cheering squad and teach the Mathematics class the proper methods of procedure. Her next book will be called "X — at Gazinkas Corner."

name: Woodman, Ruth L.
description: Ruth is a new member of the Junior class, having spent her first two years at Lasell. Ruth at first regretted her change, but of late she is perfectly satisfied with her U. N. H. choice. If anyone desires information about a certain Kappa, Ruth will gladly furnish it.

name: Woodward, Frank George
description: Everybody up! Here we have the great and honorable Business Manager of the GRANITE and a first class sheik from a Metropolis in northern New Hampshire! "Woody" superintends the B. & M. R. R. during the summer months, and we understand that he is planning on running special trains from Woodsville to Montreal this year. We all admire your "line," "Hank," and wish you the best of luck.

name: Wright, Lawrence Whitney
description: Although "Larry" is listed as an engineer, this by no means gives one an adequate idea of his true standing. He is a "Road's" scholar of note and our personal opinion from certain observations is that he seems to be in demand as a teacher in the gentle arts of skating and swimming. We hear that he has great aspirations in the singing line and devotes some evenings to this which might be used in "better" pursuits. On the whole, "Larry" is quite a Keene man.

name: Williams, Celia
description: "Sweetie" would always be so happy if she were somewhere else! We don't know whether or not she has found that mystical place, but we do know we'd be happy to have her with us. We can't imagine how Mask and Dagger continues to exist without "Sweetie's" blond good looks and her undeniable talent.

name: Wyatt, Harriett F.
motto: "Our Annabelle Lee" "She's marvelous, she's lovable," "Our little 'Harry.'" "Men just adore, none get sore," "With little 'Harry.'" "Kappa Sigma and S. A. E." "All's the same to little 'Harry.'"

name: Young, Norman H.
description: Certainly "Norm" never makes himself obnoxious by talking too much, but that might be said to be a virtue that could be observed by a great many people. He goes his own way conscientiously and quietly, and never attempts a task unless he can finish it. In hockey it can be said that he has applied this method, so we expect big things from him before he finishes college.

name: Zottu, Paul D.
description: Paul was a youth who delighted in "sharking" all his courses. He was a delight to his "profs" and, until he returned from Keene one term with a flivver and new ambitions to be a "Stepping-out boy", he always spent his spare moments in study. He seems to have anyway for he is an assistant "prof" in Physics already.
